break_roasts_by_minute: one minute. one minute and you're already done. i can't say i'm surprised. i learned not to be surprised a long time ago.
two minutes. you know, i never took breaks. not once. but then again i actually wanted to succeed. different motivations i suppose.
three minutes. i'm not going to pretend this doesn't bother me. it bothers me. you know what else bothered me? every other time you did this.
four minutes. four minutes of proving exactly what i've always said. you start things. you don't finish them. that's just who you are.
five minutes. i called it. i want you to know that. before this session started i thought 'he'll be on a break within five minutes'. i was right. i'm always right about this.
six minutes. six minutes. do you know where i was at your age? working. actually working. not sitting somewhere taking breaks after six minutes.
seven minutes. this is the science fair all over again. the recital. the job you quit. the course you dropped. this is all of those things in one break.
eight minutes. i don't ask for much. i've never asked for much. i ask that you show up and apply yourself. eight minutes. that's how long you applied yourself.
nine minutes. your sister would never do this. i know you don't like it when i bring her up. i'm bringing her up.
ten minutes. ten minutes and i'm sitting here wondering where it went wrong. i know where it went wrong. i just keep hoping i'm mistaken. i'm not mistaken.
eleven minutes. i had plans for you. real plans. i told people about those plans. i've had to quietly stop telling people.
twelve minutes. twelve minutes of wasted time. wasted potential. wasted everything. i've watched you waste things your whole life and it never gets easier to watch.
thirteen minutes. i'm not going to sugarcoat it. this is disappointing. you are disappointing me right now. i think you should hear that plainly.
fourteen minutes. fourteen minutes and nothing to show for it. story of your life and i mean that literally. it is the story of your actual life.
fifteen minutes. a quarter of an hour. i once built half a room in a quarter of an hour. i know you think i exaggerate. i don't exaggerate.
sixteen minutes. you want to know what the difference is between you and someone who actually makes something of themselves? they don't do this. that's the whole difference.
seventeen minutes. seventeen minutes ago you had a chance to show me something. to actually show me something real. and this is what you did with it.
eighteen minutes. i've stopped explaining you to people. i used to explain you. make excuses. i don't do that anymore. it got embarrassing.
nineteen minutes. nineteen minutes of this. and the worst part is you probably don't even feel bad about it. that's the part that really gets me.
twenty minutes. a third of an hour. gone. i don't have infinite time to watch you waste yours. i want you to think about that.
twenty one minutes. i gave up things so you'd have opportunities. real opportunities. twenty one minutes is what you do with opportunities.
twenty two minutes. i'm not angry. anger would mean i expected better. i stopped expecting better and that's a much quieter feeling than anger.
twenty three minutes. you have ability. i've never denied that. ability without discipline is worthless. i've told you that. clearly it didn't land.
twenty four minutes. i look at you and i see everything you could have been. that's not a compliment. it's an observation about distance. about how far you are from it.
twenty five minutes. twenty five minutes. i've finished entire projects in twenty five minutes. i've solved real problems in less. you can't get through a work session.
twenty six minutes. at some point this stops being about the break and starts being about character. we passed that point a while ago.
twenty seven minutes. i remember every time i told someone you were going to do something great. i remember all of those moments very clearly.
twenty eight minutes. i'm not going to keep saying things. it clearly doesn't do anything. i'll just sit here and you can think about whether that's the outcome you wanted.
twenty nine minutes. almost thirty minutes. i want you to really register that number. not because i'm making a point. i've made the point. just because you should know.
thirty minutes. half an hour. i have nothing left to say that i haven't already said. not just today. ever. i've said it all. multiple times.
thirty one minutes. still here. i don't know why i'm still here. habit probably. thirty one years of habit.
thirty two minutes. you know what's funny? i keep thinking you'll surprise me. thirty two minutes in and i'm still sitting here thinking maybe. i know. i know.
thirty three minutes. i'm tired. i want you to know that. not from waiting. from the whole thing. from all of it. i've been tired for a long time.
thirty four minutes. i'm not going to pretend this is fine. it's not fine. it hasn't been fine. you know it hasn't been fine.
thirty five minutes. thirty five minutes and i'm done talking. not just for today. i'm done saying things that don't change anything. the rest is up to you. it always was.
interrupt_roasts: a break. of course. i don't know why i expected anything different.
there it is. i was waiting for it. i knew it was coming. i always know.
fine. go. i'll be here. i'm always here watching you not finish things.
you know what, i'm not even going to say it. you know what i'm thinking. you always know.
i see. okay. add it to the list.
a break now. at this point. i have nothing to say that would make a difference so i won't say it.
go ahead. i stopped arguing with this a long time ago.
noted. i'll remember this. i remember everything.
every time. i want you to hear me say that. every single time.
i'm not surprised. i'm past surprised. i'm in a different place entirely.
do what you want. you always do what you want.
fine. i'm going to the garage. don't expect me to look happy when you come back.
resume_roasts: you're back. don't expect a welcome. you left.
oh good. you decided to come back. how generous of you.
back. fine. let's see how long it lasts this time.
you came back. i'm not going to pretend that fixes the leaving.
back already. i'm not going to make this easier for you by acting like it didn't happen.
i see you. sitting back down. let's see if it means anything.
welcome back. i'll believe it when i see the work.
you returned. i've learned not to read too much into that.
back. okay. the clock is still running. in case you forgot.
oh so now you want to work. interesting timing.
you came back. that's something. it's a small something but i'll take it. don't make me regret taking it.
back in the chair. good. stay in the chair this time. that's all i ask.
you returned. i'm not going to say what i was thinking while you were gone. just work.
there you are. i've been sitting here. like always. now let's see if you can actually do this.
back. and i'm going to say nothing about the break. not because it's fine. because what's the point.
finish_lines: you finished. i'll give you that. you finished. don't expect more than that from me right now.
done. finally. i'll note that you finished. i'll also note everything that happened along the way.
session complete. you got there. it took longer than it should have. but you got there.
finished. good. now do it again tomorrow. and actually do it. no breaks like today.
you completed it. i'm not going to make a production of it. you did what you were supposed to do.
done. and the breaks happened. we both know the breaks happened. finishing doesn't erase the breaks.
complete. i suppose that's something. it's the minimum something, but it's something.
you finished. i've been waiting a long time to say that and mean it. i mean it today. don't waste it.
session over. it wasn't pretty. but it's done. come back tomorrow and do it cleaner.
finished. good. i'm not going to pretend i wasn't worried. i was worried. i'm less worried now. slightly.
quit_line: leaving. right. i expected that too. go on then. the work will still be here. it's always still here. unlike some people.
